basics.name: Alex Meridian
basics.label: Computational Designer · Software Engineer · M.Arch
basics.summary: Designer and engineer working at the boundary of computation, fabrication, and built form. Seven years building parametric design tools and data pipelines for large-scale architectural practices; the last three increasingly focused on the software layer — TypeScript, WebGL, and the systems that let non-programmers author complex geometry. Comfortable in the gap between a sketch and a deployed product.

work.summary: Technical lead for a 12-person design technology unit embedded across three studio offices.
highlights: Platform: Designed and shipped a parametric massing tool used on six live projects totalling 2.4 M sq ft — reduced early-stage iteration time from weeks to hours.
Leadership: Grew the team from 3 to 12; introduced sprint cadence, code review culture, and a shared component library that cut new-tool delivery time by 40%.
Research: Led an R&D collaboration with UC Berkeley on generative structural optimisation; co-authored a paper accepted at ACADIA 2024.
Infrastructure: Migrated geometry pipeline from Rhino/Grasshopper scripts to a versioned TypeScript API, eliminating a class of reproducibility failures that had blocked two competition submissions.

work.summary: Independent practice providing computational design and software development services to architectural and engineering firms.
highlights: Clients: Delivered bespoke parametric tools and facade automation scripts for eight practices across North America and Northern Europe.
Open source: Published three Grasshopper plugins with a combined 4,200 downloads; maintained community forums and documentation.
Revenue: Grew annual revenue from zero to $180k in 18 months, entirely through referrals and conference presence.
Fabrication: Developed a curtain wall panelisation system for a 44-storey tower; coordinated with the fabricator to reduce panel count by 18% without changing the facade geometry.
Automation: Wrote Dynamo scripts that automated drawing production for a residential portfolio — saved an estimated 800 drafter-hours across the studio annually.
Mentorship: Ran monthly lunch-and-learns on computational design for a cohort of 20 architects; built internal wiki that persisted after my departure.
Competition: Core team member on an invited competition entry for a 90,000 m² cultural centre — project reached final shortlist of four.
Visualisation: Produced all project renderings and animations in-house using Rhino and V-Ray; eliminated a $60k/year external visualisation retainer.
BIM: Led the studio's migration from 2D CAD to Revit across a live hospital extension project; documented the process as a case study presented at an industry forum.
Thesis: 'Latent Tectonics' — a generative system for producing structurally rational shell geometries from voxel input fields. Awarded the Thesis Prize.
Teaching: Teaching assistant for the core computation sequence (GSD 6338) for two consecutive semesters.
Exchange: Year-long exchange at TU Delft Faculty of Architecture; studio focus on large-scale urban infrastructure.
Award: Waterloo Architecture Medal — awarded to the graduating student with the highest combined academic and studio performance.

personal.interests: Long-distance trail running, amateur radio (callsign VA3AMX), contributing to open source geometry libraries, and an ongoing project to procedurally generate plausible city plans.
